Fort Myers_FL.title: This listing isn't just a home. This is a part of Fort Myers history ...
Fort Myers_FL.caption:
This listing isn’t just a home. This is a part of Fort Myers history.

Built in 1907, this residence sits in what was prime real estate over a century ago, and it still is today. To uncover the real story behind the home, I sat down with local historian Amanda Just Bagley to learn who built it, who owned it, and why this location mattered in the early days of Fort Myers.

The home was built by Frederick Miller and purchased from John Morgan Dean, one of the key figures who helped shape Fort Myers. The historic Dean Park neighborhood is named after him, as is the Dean Building downtown, which originally served as the city’s bank.

Just steps from what was once Millionaires Row, the home is walkable to downtown and tucked into Dean Park, known for its palm lined streets and timeless character.

The current owner has taken great care to restore the home while preserving its historic charm. The layout spans three floors. The main level features two separate seating areas, a spacious dine in kitchen, a cigar room, and office space overlooking the yard. The second floor is dedicated to the bedrooms and bathrooms, while the third floor offers a bonus space designed for fun or flexibility.

Some homes are just addresses. This one comes with a legacy.
The next chapter gets written by whoever gets it next.

#HistoricFortMyers #DeanPark #CenturyHome #OldFloridaCharm #HistoricHomes #FortMyersRealEstate #SWFLRealEstate #WalkableDowntown #LuxuryWithHistory #SERHANTSWFL
Fort Myers_FL.fullName: Krista Fogelsong Realtor/On Camera Confidence Coach
Fort Myers_FL.script: This house was built in 1907 and most people drive right past it without knowing what they're looking at. It sits on First Street, steps from the Caloosahatchee River and walking distance to downtown Fort Myers. This was literally called Millionaires' Road because the most prominent businessmen in the city came here to build mansions. Some of those original homes still stand today. This one has three floors, a cigar room, original character you simply cannot replicate with new construction, and a location that has been desirable for over a hundred years. I've sold homes all over Fort Myers and properties with this kind of history, this kind of location, they don't come around often. When they do, they go fast. Are you someone who wants a home with a real story behind it, or are you strictly looking for new construction? Drop your answer below and I'll show you what's available in each category right now.

$350K in Southwest Florida can mean very different homes depending on where you buy.

In Fort Myers, you’re often around the median price for a single-family home.
In Cape Coral, you might find a newer build with upgrades.
In Estero, $350K usually means a condo or townhome in a gated community.
And in Alva, you might get more land and that quiet Old Florida feel.

Same budget. Completely different lifestyle.

Curious what’s actually available right now around $350K in SWFL?

Comment “350K” or send a DM and I’ll share a few options.

Fort Myers_FL.fullName: Jessica Weathers Nolan | Southwest Florida Real Estate
Fort Myers_FL.script: Here's something nobody tells you when you're searching homes with a $350K budget in Southwest Florida. The same money buys completely different lives depending on where you land. In Fort Myers, $350K is right around median, so you're getting a solid three-bed, two-bath in an established neighborhood, maybe 1,400 to 1,700 square feet with a yard. Cape Coral gives you something newer at that price, sometimes a newer roof, better finishes, maybe 1,800 square feet. Estero runs higher overall, so $350K there gets you a condo or villa instead of a single-family home. And Alva? That same budget could put you on half an acre with real privacy and that old Florida feel. Four cities, four completely different lifestyles, same exact budget. So here's my question for you: which one of those actually sounds like your life? Drop it in the comments and I'll show you exactly what's available this week.

If you want to understand what’s actually happening in Naples luxury real estate right now, watch the $7M+ market this week.

Active= New Inventory

4951 Gulf Shore Blvd N 703, Park Shore $7.95M

Pending = Buyer Demand

6897 Grenadier Blvd 1202, Pelican Bay $8.45M
105 Felipe Ln, Barefoot Beach $8.5M
2818 Thistle Way, Grey Oaks $10.5M

1731 Gordon Dr, Aqualane Shores $24.995M
1601 Gulf Shore Blvd N Penthouse 1, Coquina Sands $35M

Sold = Price Discovery
11125 Gulf Shore Dr 605, Vanderbilt Beach Sold $6.9M | ~$1,725/sq ft
221 Aqua Ct, Aqualane Shores Sold $10.45M | ~$1,752/sq ft
661 Galleon Dr, Port Royal Sold $16.75M | ~$3,350/sq ft
3520 Gin Ln, Port Royal Sold $17.275M | ~$2,204/sq ft

Inventory is building, but so is selectivity. Buyers are still transacting, just not across the board. The spread from ~$1,700 to over $3,300 per square foot is the clearest signal, value is no longer tied to price point alone, it’s tied to precision. 

Waterfront quality, view, elevation, and exact positioning are driving decisions, and Port Royal continues to operate in its own tier.

This isn’t a slowdown, it’s a filter. Buyers are still decisive when a property feels irreplaceable and far more disciplined when it doesn’t.

Cheena Chandra, a luxury real estate advisor selling Naples waterfront and coveted homes and neighborhoods that match how you want to live.

Builders right now are basically PAYING you to buy. We're talking rate buydowns, closing cost credits, free upgrades. They have inventory sitting and they need to move it before quarter end.

New construction also means no bidding wars, no inspection nightmares, no surprise repairs. You pick your finishes and you're done.

So real question for you... have you actually toured a new construction community in Florida yet or are you just watching from the sidelines?

USA.title: Curious how experts see the 2026 housing market shaping up ...
USA.script:
Here is the script:

Wait... experts are calling for a 14% jump in home sales in 2026 and most buyers have zero idea this is coming.

Here is what that actually means for YOU right now.

When mortgage rates drop toward 6%, millions of buyers who got priced out suddenly qualify again. All at once. That is not a slow trickle. History shows us these recoveries always happen fast and big, never slow and small.

So if you are sitting on the fence thinking you have all the time in the world... you might be planning your move right when everyone else is making theirs.

The people who win in real estate are the ones who move before the crowd figures it out.

I work with buyers in this market every single day and I can tell you exactly what this forecast means for your specific neighborhood.

So tell me... are you planning to buy or sell in the next 12 months?

USA.caption:
The 2026 housing market is already starting to feel different, especially in Long Beach and Orange County. Buyer motivation is returning in a meaningful way, and many people who have been waiting for the right moment are finally ready to make a move. This shift is happening alongside gradually improving interest rates and new leadership expected at the Fed this May, which has many watching closely for what comes next.

At the same time, more homes are expected to hit the market. Increased inventory means more choices, healthier negotiations, and a more balanced real estate environment than what we have seen over the past several years. Whether you are buying or selling, understanding these changes early can give you a real advantage.

If you are planning a move in 2026, now is the time to get informed and prepared. This is not the same market we have been navigating since 2020.

What are your predictions for the housing market this year? Drop a comment below.

Here's the truth nobody's talking about right now.

Everyone's obsessed with interest rates but that's actually NOT the biggest story heading into 2026.

What I'm seeing on the ground in Long Beach and Orange County is way more interesting.

Motivated buyers are coming back. Like actually motivated. That hasn't happened in years.

Rates are slowly coming down. New Fed leadership hits in May and insiders are already whispering about more cuts coming.

AND inventory is finally opening up. Real options. Real choices. The kind of market where you can actually negotiate again.

This is that window. The one people look back on and say "I wish I moved on that."

The buyers and sellers who act in the next 90 days are going to look like geniuses by end of year.

So tell me something... are you waiting for perfect or are you ready to actually make a move this year?

USA.caption:
2026 is shaping up to be a pivotal year in real estate. 🏡

@therealjasonabrams is watching three pressure points:

1️⃣ Interest rates: They jumped from 3% to nearly 8%. If they keep trending down, demand shifts.

2️⃣ First-time buyers: The average age is 40. If affordability improves, does the next wave step in?

3️⃣ Mortgage lock-ins: Millions sit on 3% rates. If rates ease, does inventory unlock?

What are you watching in 2026? Comment below.
USA.author: kellerwilliamsrealty
USA.title: 2026 is shaping up to be a pivotal year in real estate ...
USA.script:
2026 is about to shake up real estate in a BIG way and here are the 3 things you need to know right now.

Rates. They hit almost 8% back in 2023 and have been slowly dropping. More cuts could be coming this year. That matters for your wallet.

First time buyers. The average age just hit 40. FORTY. As the market softens that number should start dropping again. Meaning more competition for starter homes.

And the big one. Mortgage lock-in. Millions of homeowners are sitting on 3% rates feeling completely stuck. But life happens. New jobs. Babies. Divorces. And when rates drop enough those homes flood the market.

Here is what that means for YOU. More inventory. More opportunity. Possibly your best window in years to buy or sell.

So tell me honestly, are you waiting for rates to drop before you make your move?
